title: The Macro Minute:  Can risk assets achieve escape velocity without QE?

Happy Thursday out there, team 42. It's
your skipper here at Darius Dell to
present our macro minute for Thursday,
April 24th, 2025. As always, we'll start
with the executive summary from today's
leadoff morning note. If you would like
to review the analysis supporting these
conclusions and to see if you need to
make any changes in your portfolio to
remain on the right side of market risk,
you need to be a client of 42 macro. So
today's key macro question is, can risk
assets achieve escape velocity without
QE? Our answers are all is going
according to plan as it pertains to our
outlooks for policy and asset markets.
We still think a retest of the lows in
stocks is likely given the pending
growth slowdown that will be realized in
the second and third quarter hard data
and reaffirmed throughout Q2 earning
season this summer. Moreover, we are
still in a global debt refinancing air
pocket. That said, we continue to
believe investors should buy the retest
dip with two hands because we struggle
to see how the stock market makes new
lows given our core thesis that the bond
market broke President Trump on April
9th. That remains our northstar and we
are keen to follow the drinking gore.
So, the answer to today's key macro
question is yes, but with a caveat. We
must continue to be right on our core
thesis that the bond market broke
President Trump on April 9th. While it's
easy for most firms that do what we do
to cop out by labeling Trump a quote
unquote wild card, we actually
understand that investors get paid to
take risk and not wait around for
certainty in financial markets. Markets
will have moved by the time investors
gain true certainty on the policy front.
It is unlikely Bitcoin retest its lows
because the growth slowdown will pull
forward the timing of Fed easing, which
is a bigger upside risk for the crypto
market than it is for the stock market
at the current juncture. Given the
precondition of overexposure to US
equities among institutional investors,
many of whom have yet to make
significant reallocations to their home
and international markets and will if
the dollarbear market continues. We
think it will. Trust in the currency
market is earned, not allocated, and US
policy makers have a long way to go to
regain the valuation premiums that were
priced into the US dollar and broader US
assets. Transition to our 42 micro
dashboard. As always, we'll wrap up with
a question from our community. This
one's titled, "Hi DD, new subscriber
here. Very excited to be a part of 42
macro. Appreciate you." Says, "I have a
newbie question for you. Should I be
rebalancing my wings as they change or
should uh or should I just buy the
initial allocation and not touch it
again until instructed otherwise? For
example, if I purchase 30% gold, but the
price keeps going up and that makes my
gold allocation higher in the initial
30% uh waiting, say go say it climbs to
35%. Should I be selling off 5% to get
back to the 30% or let it ride until
KISS changes its allocation? Hope that
makes sense and look forward to your
answer. So, uh thank you for joining us.
I appreciate you as we appreciate uh
many of the uh uh I want to say maybe
couple thousand new clients there. So,
uh we say we appreciate you guys uh for
joining us. Uh we're kicking butt and
taking names and adding a tremendous
amount of value for our clients in not
only in terms of helping their
portfolios navigate this forth turning
poly crisis, but also in a in a way that
helps them reduce stress, in a way that
helps them reduce the amount of time
that they spend, you know, trying to
manage their portfolio. And ultimately,
as we talked about with the with the
kind compliment that the um uh uh
subscriber gave us, I want to say on
Monday or Tuesday of this week, you
know, we're allowing them to get back to
what they want to do in life, which is
spend time with their families, you
know, grow their businesses, you know,
pursue their passions and hobbies as
opposed to spending all day worrying
about liquidity and earnings and
everything else. That's what Kiss and
Dr. Mo do. We remove the amount of time
that you spend for investing and give it
back to you so you can go focus on other
things. Um and so our clients um you
know really really uh are benefiting
from that. We're grateful that they are
benefiting from that. We appreciate each
and every one of them. So getting back
into uh the question u the uh so this
question just like every other question
as it relates to how to implement KISS
or how to respond to Dr. Mo signals KISS
for retail Dr. Mo for institutions is
covered in our uh KISS portfolio
construction process FAQ and our
discretionary risk management overlay
FAQ. So, you can access that as a 42
micro client here in this hamburger
menu. Uh, the KISS FAQ will just take
you to that and take you right to the
question that the uh that the client is
asking. Um, let me see here. Oh, here we
go. Do you recommend rebalancing your
portfolio each time KISS signals a
change in actual exposure? So, let me
just open that up and read the answer.
Says, yes, the purpose of KISS is
three-fold. Number one, to help
investors block out the noise and remain
invested heading in heading into and
throughout bull markets. Number two, to
help investors block out the noise and
remain uninvested heading into and
throughout bare markets. And number
three, to outperform the traditional
60/40 portfolio. Let me repeat that
again. The purpose of KISS is
three-fold. Number one, to help
investors block out the noise and remain
invested heading in into and throughout
bare markets. Number two, to help
investors block out the noise and remain
uninvested heading into and throughout
bare markets. And number three, to
outperform the 6040 portfolio. Block out
the noise. Notice I said that multiple
times. Block out the noise and get
yourself some time back so you can
reallocate that time to your kids, to
your wife, to your husband, to your
family, to your mom, to your dad, to
your community, to your church, to
everything but spending all day on
Twitter worrying about liquidity. How
about that? Kiss can only help investors
accomplish these three goals if they
dutifully implement each pivot by dollar
cost averaging in response to buy
signals and selling quickly in response
to sell signals. Over time, either the
size of the SPLG, the stock market,
GLDM, gold, and FBTC uh Bitcoin
positions may grow or decline in size
relative to to the most recent actual
exposure publication due to market
performance. Thus, rebalancing is
required at regular intervals to
minimize tracking error. There is no
right answer for how best to rebalance.
Much like we talked about earlier this
week as well, there's no right answer
for how best to dollar cost averaging.
And that's because each investor has a
different tax situation and bandwidth
for investing. Not everybody has time to
spend all day dollar cost averaging or
rebalancing. Some people do, some people
don't. And so the strategy that I employ
as 42 macro founder and CEO. The
strategy that I employ to rebalance my
portfolio each time KISS signals a
change in actual exposure uh in one or
more of the ETFs. Um per the out of
sample back test. There's been only
about two trades a month uh in KISS per
January 2018. Uh that it creates the two
opportunities to rebalance per month uh
if market performance warrants
rebalance. Another strategy would be to
rebalance on a calendar-based frequency,
i.e. at the end of the week, the month
or quarter. Again, there are no right
answers for rebalancing. Investors
should explore multiple options and
settle on what works best for them. So
again, I rebalance every time we get a
new signal in KISS, of which there's on
average about two changes per month
according to the um to the to the latest
KISS back test. Um so just be aware of
that. Um you know, shifting gears a
little bit here. I just want to make
sure investors are aware of the types of
questions that we get in our community
chat that we have, you know, created
answers uh uh for you guys here in our
KISS construction process FAQ. So, let
me just read them um go down the list
and just read them. We're not going to
uh read the answers to all of them, but
I want to make sure investors are aware
uh both inside and outside of our payw
wall uh that we have answered many of
these questions already. So, number one,
what is the KISS portfolio construction
process? Number two, how is the KISS
portfolio performed in comparison to the
traditional 6040 portfolio? Uh, number
three, uh, who is the KISS portfolio
construction process for? Uh, number
four, or I need to keep numbering them.
Uh, what if I'm an institutional
investor and do not wish to implement
the KISS portfolio? Uh, what are the
allocations of the KISS portfolio? Uh,
well, which ETFs are chosen? Uh, sorry,

portfolio in terms of where to find it
in the research and in the dashboard?
Uh, which ETFs are chosen for each asset
class? What strategy does KISS employ to
maximize upside capture in bull markets
and minimize downside capture in bare
markets? Will I get notified of changes
regarding uh the KISS portfolio
allocations? Do you recommend
rebalancing your portfolio each time
KISS signals a change in actual
exposure? What if I'm a new member and
need to get allocated to a fully
invested or near fully invested KISS
from scratch? What if I want to
implement the KISS portfolio, but I
prefer other exposures instead of the
default stocks gold and Bitcoin? Uh if I
implement a customized version of KISS,
how do I calculate the exposure to my
preferred ETFs? How do I reconcile
conflicting signals between KISS and Dr.
Mo? How do I quit? How quickly should I
respond in my portfolio each time KISS
signals a change in actual exposure? And
lastly, what if the current KISS
allocations seem in congruent with 42
macros fundamental research summary or
quantitative risk management summary? So
again, you know, we have thousands of
clients around the world all
participating in KISS or many of whom
are participating in KISS. And so
obviously we want to make sure that
investors really understand uh uh the
the the tool so that they can maximize
the value that the tool creates. You
know, again, I use KISS to manage my
entire liquid net worth. I don't have
any other positions in my portfolio as
an institutional investor other than
stocks, gold, Bitcoin, and cash at any
time ever. Why? Because KISS works. I
understand the value of KISS. We have
thousands of clients around the world
that understand the value of KISS. And
so, we want to make sure that investors
understand how to best implement KISS on
their on their behalf so that they can

that the tool creates. And so we
